---
title: "Home"
page-layout: full
---

::: {#top .hero-shell}
[]{.eyebrow} Technical case study with product polish

# Pythia { .hero-title }

::: {.hero-subtitle}
Data Science for Cafeterias

Final project for the Master in Data Science at UCM/NTIC.
:::

::: {.badge-row}
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Real world project</strong>
<span>Uses real sales and customer review data from a Madrid coffee shop to build a practical, intelligent cafeteria application.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Integrates distinct fields of knowledge</strong>
<span>Effectively combines coursework in ETL, EDA, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Time Series, NLP, and advanced visualization into one coherent solution.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Built as a real product</strong>
<span>Presented as a functioning MVP in Streamlit, showing the project as a usable product rather than only an academic deliverable.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Recognitions</strong>
<span>Received the maximum grade and earned 2nd place in a pitch contest.</span>
:::
:::

::: {.cta-row}
[Watch Demo](https://youtu.be/gkD9bKw1ikY){.cta-button .cta-tertiary}
:::
:::

## Project Details {#project-details .section-title}

Pythia is designed to be readable in one sitting: start with the business challenge, move through the analytical pipeline, review the modeling choices, and finish with measurable impact.

::: {.detail-grid}
::: {.content-card .detail-card .simple-card}
### Business Problem

Pythia starts from a real decision-making challenge: how to convert available data into reliable guidance that supports faster, better-informed action in a cafeteria context.

**Why this matters**

Organizations often have operational data but lack a trustworthy way to translate it into practical day-to-day decisions.

**The gap**

Raw information alone does not create value. What is needed is a structured analytical workflow that turns data into usable insight.

**Pythia's contribution**

Pythia combines business framing, technical rigor, and clear presentation so the outputs connect naturally with real-world action.
:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Data and Methodology

The methodology is designed to be technically sound and easy to audit. Each phase contributes to trust: data provenance, preprocessing decisions, evaluation design, and transparent interpretation.

**Core components**

- Data sources and scope
- Cleaning and transformation
- Feature engineering
- Evaluation setup
- Interpretation and limitations

**What this section should show**

- data pipeline and dataset scope
- acquisition, quality checks, and preprocessing
- train-validation-test strategy
- reproducibility choices and repository structure

::: {.media-slot}
Space for data pipeline graphic, EDA snapshot, or methodology table
:::
:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Modeling

The modeling section should communicate technical judgment and restraint: why the chosen approaches fit the problem, what alternatives were considered, and how performance was balanced with interpretability and robustness.

**Review focus**

- baselines before complex models
- rationale for model selection
- tuning without overfitting
- explainability and business usefulness

::: {.media-slot}
Space for model comparison chart, feature importance graphic, or architecture diagram
:::
:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Results and Impact

Results should show more than model metrics. Pythia is strongest when the performance story is connected to user value, decision quality, and operational improvement.

**Evidence to include**

- final metrics table
- comparison with baselines
- practical impact on decisions
- communication impact and recognition

::: {.media-slot}
Space for results table, performance chart, or impact dashboard
:::
:::
:::

## Video Demo {#video-demo .section-title}

::: {.youtube-frame}
<iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/gkD9bKw1ikY" title="Pythia demo video" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
:::

## Team {#team .section-title}

Pythia was shaped by a collaborative team with a shared focus on technical quality, product thinking, and clear communication.

::: {.team-grid}
::: {.team-card}
![Team member 1](../assets/images/team/team-member-1.svg){.team-photo}
### Team Member One
Data science, experimentation, and analytical framing.
:::
::: {.team-card}
![Team member 2](../assets/images/team/team-member-2.svg){.team-photo}
### Team Member Two
Modeling, technical validation, and implementation.
:::
::: {.team-card}
![Team member 3](../assets/images/team/team-member-3.svg){.team-photo}
### Team Member Three
Product narrative, presentation, and stakeholder communication.
:::
:::

## Awards {#awards .section-title}

The project combined strong technical work with strong communication, and the outcomes reflect both.

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.award-card}
### Maximum grade
Pythia received the highest academic evaluation for the final project.
:::
::: {.award-card}
### 2nd place in pitch contest
The project also stood out in a presentation setting, validating its clarity, storytelling, and practical positioning.
:::
::: {.award-card}
### Recruiter-ready presentation
The combination of product polish and technical depth makes the project especially effective for hiring conversations.
:::
:::

## Contact {#contact .section-title}

For opportunities, technical discussion, or a walkthrough of the project, reach out through the channels below.

::: {.contact-grid}
::: {.contact-card}
### LinkedIn
[linkedin.com/in/your-linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/your-linkedin/)
:::
::: {.contact-card}
### GitHub
[github.com/your-username/pythia](https://github.com/your-username/pythia)
:::
::: {.contact-card}
### Email
[your.email@example.com](mailto:your.email@example.com)
:::
:::

Pythia  
Master’s Final Project at UCM  
Data Science  
Available for technical interviews, project walkthroughs, and collaboration conversations
